question_text: A light bulb transfers 100 J of electrical energy into 15 J of light energy and 85 J of heat energy. Which statement best describes what happens to the energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy is lost because only 15 J becomes useful light

options.label: B
options.text: Energy is destroyed as heat so the total is not conserved

options.label: C
options.text: All 100 J is transferred into different forms of energy; none is lost

options.label: D
options.text: The heat energy proves the light bulb is inefficient and breaks the conservation law

explanation: Energy conservation means energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred from one form to another. The 100 J input becomes 15 J of light and 85 J of heat—the total is still 100 J, so energy is conserved. The heat is 'wasted' energy in terms of usefulness, but it still exists.
